description: A designed ICE legal-careers recruitment poster. The image is a warmly-lit, golden-toned photograph of a bronze Lady Justice statue (blindfolded, holding aloft a set of balance scales) standing in what reads as a sunlit law-library or courthouse interior with blurred shelves and light beams behind her. In the upper-left corner is the U.S. Department of Homeland Security seal beside the agency wordmark 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement' and the URL 'ICE.gov/LegalCareers' in dark blue. Across the lower-left in a large serif font, white text reads 'Not a career.' followed by a bigger, bolder 'A calling.' The composition is elegant and aspirational rather than aggressive. The propaganda/messaging technique frames working as an ICE attorney (Office of the Principal Legal Advisor) as a noble vocation tied to justice, supported by the tweet's pitch of 'hiring bonuses up to $50,000' and 'six-figure salary.' Target audience: prospective government lawyers / law graduates and attorneys being recruited into ICE's legal division.
notable_text: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement. ICE.gov/LegalCareers. Not a career. A calling.
